page: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The processor is equipped with 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It includes hardware-level instruction sets such as AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI), and Intel Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) cryptography.

This server configuration is provisioned with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, though it is premium storage capable for attaching external block storage. The absence of local NVMe storage requires external disk attachment for persistent data, which is a key architectural tradeoff for storage-heavy workloads.

Performance benchmarks place the DC16s_v3 consistently in the average range for most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance registers at 30,343.46, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 13,843.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 1,687.72 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 60.51 GB/sec. In specialized tasks, the server demonstrates strong performance in Geekbench image processing (13,806.0) and small-model LLM text generation (316.58 tokens/sec). However, it exhibits weak performance for large-scale LLM inference, processing only 6.61 tokens/sec on a 70B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio make this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local scratch disks. It is well-suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external premium storage for data persistence. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 30,808.33 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 13,843.0. It shows strong performance in image processing and small-model text generation, but exhibits weak throughput for large 70B LLM inference. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, software compilation, and web serving where dedicated CPU resources and high memory density are required without the need for local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering reliable performance for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC16s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and premium storage support for database workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 16-core Intel Xeon virtual machine with 128 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and premium external storage configurations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
No local storage, requiring external premium storage attachment
Strong performance in image processing and small-model text generation
Average performance across standard database and multi-core benchmarks
Suitable for memory-intensive workloads, software compilation, and web serving
